title: Fix Persistent Login, Logout Button, and Unauthorized Submission Error

requirements.summary: Implement persistent customer session in AuthContext so that once a customer logs in, their session is restored automatically on every page load using localStorage and they are not redirected to the login screen unless they explicitly log out
acceptanceCriteria: After a customer logs in, refreshing or reopening the app does not show the login screen.
The customer session (user ID, name, mobile, role) is saved to localStorage on successful login.
On app load, AuthContext reads localStorage and restores the customer session if a valid entry exists.
The customer is only sent to the login screen after an explicit logout action.

file_operations.description: Add automatic session restoration on mount by reading localStorage on AuthProvider initialization. Ensure that when a valid customer session exists in localStorage, it is loaded into state immediately so the user is not redirected to login. Update the customerLogin function to save the full customer session (customerId, name, mobile, role='customer') to localStorage immediately after successful login. Ensure the useEffect hook restores this session on component mount before any navigation logic runs.

Update the screen navigation state machine to check AuthContext.customer before showing the login screen. If a customer session exists (restored from localStorage by AuthContext), skip the login/selection screens and navigate directly to the appropriate authenticated screen (Home or the last visited screen). Ensure the splash screen transitions to Home (or the main dashboard) when a valid customer session is present, rather than forcing the user back to LoginSelection.

requirements.summary: Fix the Logout button in the Layout component so that clicking it clears the customer or admin session from both AuthContext state and localStorage, then navigates the user back to the login/selection screen
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Logout button clears the user session from AuthContext and localStorage.
After logout, the app navigates to the login/selection screen (LoginSelection or CustomerLogin).
The Logout button is visible and functional for both customer and admin roles.
After logout, no stale session data remains — the user cannot navigate back to protected screens without re-authenticating.

file_operations.description: Wire the existing Logout button to call the appropriate logout function from AuthContext (customerLogout or adminLogout depending on the active role). After calling logout, use the onNavigate callback to navigate back to the LoginSelection screen. Ensure the button is visible and functional for both customer and admin users. Add a confirmation prompt if desired, or implement immediate logout with visual feedback.

Ensure the customerLogout function clears both the customer state (setCustomer(null)) and removes the customer session from localStorage (localStorage.removeItem). Similarly, ensure adminLogout clears admin state and localStorage. Add clear error handling so that logout always succeeds even if localStorage operations fail. After logout, the context should return to an unauthenticated state with no stale session data.

Add logic to detect when the user session (customer or admin) has been cleared (e.g., by checking AuthContext.customer === null and AuthContext.admin === null) and automatically transition the screen state back to 'loginSelection' or 'customerLogin'. This ensures that after logout is called from Layout, the app navigation resets to the login flow without requiring a full page refresh.

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Unauthorized: Only authenticated users can submit orders' error in ServiceForm and any other form submission flows by ensuring that after a customer logs in, the authenticated user's customer ID is correctly attached to every backend call (submitOrder and any similar mutations in useQueries.ts), so forms like 'Niwas Praman Patra' submit successfully without an unauthorized error
acceptanceCriteria: A logged-in customer can submit any service form (including Niwas Praman Patra) without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
The customer's ID or session token is read from AuthContext and passed correctly in the submitOrder backend call.

The backend actor's submitOrder (or equivalent) function accepts the authenticated customer's ID supplied from the frontend session, not relying solely on Internet Identity principal when the app uses OTP-based custom auth.
If the session is missing or expired at submission time, the user is redirected to login with a clear message instead of showing a raw 'Unauthorized' error.

file_operations.description: Update the useSubmitOrder mutation hook to accept the customerId as a required parameter from the caller (ServiceForm). Ensure the mutation function reads the customerId from AuthContext and passes it to the backend actor.submitOrder call. If customerId is missing or null, throw a user-friendly error ('Please log in to submit orders') instead of allowing the backend to return 'Unauthorized'. Add error handling to detect backend authorization failures and provide clear feedback to the user.

Update the form submission handler to read the current customer session from AuthContext (using useContext(AuthContext)) and extract the customerId. Pass this customerId to the submitOrder mutation from useQueries.ts. Before submission, check if the customer session exists; if not, display an error message ('You must be logged in to submit this form') and optionally redirect the user to the login screen. Ensure that the customer's name and mobile are also pulled from the session to pre-fill or validate form fields as needed.

Ensure the AuthContext customer object always includes a valid customerId field (the customer's mobile number or unique identifier) after successful login. This field must be accessible to all form components that need to submit authenticated requests. Add a helper function or getter (e.g., getCustomerId()) if needed to make it easier for components to retrieve the authenticated customer ID without null-checking boilerplate.
